# 99. Prometheus/Grafana stack installer removed

**Date:** 2026-06-22

**Optional per env:** _only required on some environments_


## CommCare Version Dependency
This change is not known to be dependent on any particular version of CommCare.


## Change Context
`commcare-cloud` no longer includes a playbook to install the
Prometheus/Grafana monitoring stack. Operators who want Prometheus can stand
it up independently and point it at CommCare HQ's `/metrics` endpoint.

## Details
Dimagi has not run a Prometheus/Grafana stack internally for a while now,
which means we're not exercising the deployment automation in our own
environments. An untested installer creates false confidence and is likely to
break quietly as upstream roles and exporters drift. Removing it feels more
honest than shipping something we can't stand behind.

`deploy_prometheus.yml` and all associated Ansible roles have been removed
from `commcare-cloud`.

CommCare HQ's own Prometheus integration (controlled by
`prometheus_monitoring_enabled: true`) remains intact. When enabled, HQ
exposes `/metrics` via a multiprocess aggregation process. See the
[monitoring docs](https://commcare-cloud.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operations/3-monitoring.html#prometheus)
for guidance on installing the Prometheus stack and service exporters.

## Steps to update
If your per-environment `prometheus.yml` file contains
`prometheus_monitoring_enabled: true`, move that setting to `public.yml`
first, then delete `prometheus.yml` (HQ's metrics endpoint will be disabled if
you delete the file without migrating the setting).

The HQ `/metrics` endpoint is unchanged — existing Prometheus installations
will continue working without reconfiguration. Reference the
[monitoring docs](https://commcare-cloud.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operations/3-monitoring.html#prometheus)
for suggestions for ongoing maintenance of your Prometheus stack.
